metadata.description:
Build the evaluation/analysis flow infrastructure for the factorial design: CV-average MLflow pyfunc, factorial ensemble strategies (within-family, cross-family, all-trainable, all+zero-shot), hierarchical evaluation with UQ metrics, and cross-fold consistency checks.
This PR creates the l=5 ensemble factor and f=2 fold strategy factor.

references.ref: Lakshminarayanan et al. (2017). "Simple and Scalable Predictive Uncertainty." NeurIPS.

metadata.existing_infrastructure: analysis_flow.py: 4 ensemble strategies (per_loss_single_best, etc.) mlflow_wrapper.py: MiniVessSegModel + MiniVessEnsembleModel pyfunc ensemble/builder.py: EnsembleBuilder with checkpoint discovery ensemble/strategies.py: EnsemblePredictor (mean, majority_vote, weighted) ensemble/deep_ensembles.py: DeepEnsemblePredictor with UQ decomposition evaluation_runner.py: UnifiedEvaluationRunner with hierarchical dataloaders

phase.name: CV-Average Fold Prediction Averaging
phase.description: CV-average is NOT an ensemble — it is the standard 3-fold cross-validation inference mode. For a single condition (model+loss+aux_calib), it loads the 3 fold-specific checkpoints, runs inference on each, and averages the softmax predictions. This requires a custom MLflow pyfunc class.

task.name: CV-average MLflow pyfunc class
task.description:
Create MiniVessCVAverageModel(mlflow.pyfunc.PythonModel) that: 1. Loads 3 fold-specific checkpoints from a single MLflow parent run 2. Runs inference on each fold's model 3. Averages softmax predictions across folds 4. Returns averaged prediction + fold-level disagreement as UQ signal
The class is registered in MLflow Model Registry for serving via BentoML.
Input: (B, 1, D, H, W) volume Output: { "prediction": (B, 2, D, H, W) mean softmax, "fold_disagreement": (B, 1, D, H, W) std across folds }
CRITICAL: This is model-agnostic — it wraps any ModelAdapter via config. The fold checkpoints are discovered from MLflow tags (checkpoint_dir_fold_N).
task.acceptance_criteria: - 3 forward passes produce averaged prediction - Identical fold models produce zero disagreement - Different fold models produce nonzero disagreement - MLflow ModelSignature matches MiniVessEnsembleModel - Single-fold graceful fallback (no error, just uses that fold) - Model-agnostic via config (works with DynUNet, Mamba++, SAM3)

task.name: Fold strategy: single-best-fold selection
task.description:
Implement select_best_fold() that picks the fold with the best compound metric for a given condition. The compound metric is:
compound = 0.5 * clDice + 0.5 * normalize(MASD)
where normalize(MASD) = 1 - (MASD - MASD_min) / (MASD_max - MASD_min) to convert MASD (lower=better) to [0,1] (higher=better).
This is the f=2 fold strategy factor: - single-best-fold: use this function - cv-average: use MiniVessCVAverageModel (T1)
Returns the fold_id with the best compound score.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Returns integer fold_id (0, 1, or 2) - Compound metric is 0.5*clDice + 0.5*(1 - normalized_MASD) - MASD normalization uses min/max across folds - Ties broken deterministically (lower fold_id wins)

phase.name: Factorial Ensemble Strategies
phase.description:
Implement the l=5 ensemble factor. The existing EnsembleBuilder has 4 strategies but they don't match the factorial design. We need: l=0: None (single model, no ensembling) l=1: Within-family (same model, different losses) l=2: Cross-family (different models, same loss) l=3: All-trainable (4 models x 3 losses) l=4: All+zero-shot (all trainable + SAM3 Vanilla + VesselFM)
Each ensemble operates ON TOP of the fold strategy (single-best or cv-average).

task.name: Factorial ensemble builder
task.description:
Create FactorialEnsembleBuilder that produces ensemble specifications for all l=5 levels. Unlike the existing EnsembleBuilder (which groups by loss), this builder understands the factorial structure.
within_family_ensemble(model="dynunet", fold_strategy="cv_average"): → DynUNet×dice_ce + DynUNet×cbdice_cldice + DynUNet×dice_ce_cldice → 3 member models (or 9 if single-fold × 3 folds)
cross_family_ensemble(loss="cbdice_cldice", fold_strategy="single_best"): → DynUNet + Mamba++ + SAM3_TopoLoRA + SAM3_Hybrid, all with cbdice_cldice → 4 member models
all_trainable_ensemble(fold_strategy="cv_average"): → All 12 condition models (4 models × 3 losses) → 12 member models (each is CV-average of 3 folds)
all_plus_zeroshot(fold_strategy="cv_average"): → All 12 + SAM3 Vanilla + VesselFM → 14 member models

tdd_spec.tests: test_within_family_ensemble_member_count test_within_family_ensemble_same_model test_cross_family_ensemble_member_count test_cross_family_ensemble_same_loss test_all_trainable_ensemble_count test_all_plus_zeroshot_ensemble_count test_none_strategy_single_model test_ensemble_with_cv_average_fold_strategy test_ensemble_with_single_best_fold_strategy test_ensemble_spec_names

task.acceptance_criteria: - Within-family: 3 members (1 model × 3 losses) - Cross-family: 4 members (4 models × 1 loss) - All-trainable: 12 members (4 × 3) - All+zero-shot: 14 members (12 + 2) - None: 1 member (single model) - Each ensemble has descriptive name for MLflow tagging - Works with both fold strategies

task.name: Hierarchical ensemble MLflow pyfunc
task.description:
Create MiniVessHierarchicalEnsembleModel that wraps multiple CV-average models into a single ensemble. This is "2 MLflow ensembles in succession" as the user described:
Level 1: Each member is a MiniVessCVAverageModel (3-fold average) Level 2: Ensemble averages across Level 1 members
Example: within-family ensemble of DynUNet with cv-average fold strategy = average( cv_avg(DynUNet×dice_ce, 3 folds), cv_avg(DynUNet×cbdice_cldice, 3 folds), cv_avg(DynUNet×dice_ce_cldice, 3 folds) )
Output includes full UQ decomposition: - total_uncertainty: entropy of mean prediction - aleatoric: mean per-member entropy - epistemic: mutual information (total - aleatoric)
task.acceptance_criteria: - Two-level hierarchy works (CV-avg members → ensemble average) - UQ decomposition matches Lakshminarayanan et al. formulas - Single member degenerates to CV-average (no ensemble overhead) - Prediction shape matches (B, 2, D, H, W) - MLflow signature compatible with serving

phase.name: Evaluation Pipeline Extensions
phase.description: Extend the evaluation runner to handle the full factorial evaluation matrix: 216 checkpoints × 2 fold strategies × 2 recalibration × 5 ensemble = 4,320 points.

task.name: Factorial evaluation orchestrator
task.acceptance_criteria: - Correct total: 4,320 evaluation points (with some invalid combos skipped) - Each point tagged with all factorial factors - Resume capability (skip already-evaluated points) - Per-volume metrics logged (not just aggregates)

task.name: UQ metric logging per volume
task.description:
Extend evaluation to log per-volume uncertainty quantification metrics alongside segmentation metrics. For each volume, log:
- uq/total_uncertainty: H[p_mean] (entropy of mean prediction) - uq/aleatoric: E[H[p_m]] (mean per-member entropy, ensemble only) - uq/epistemic: MI = total - aleatoric (ensemble only) - uq/fold_disagreement: std across fold predictions (cv-average only) - uq/calibration_ece: per-volume ECE (from calibration.py)
These metrics feed into the biostatistics flow for UQ analysis. For single models (no ensemble), only total_uncertainty and ECE are logged.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Single model: total_uncertainty + ECE logged - Ensemble: all 5 UQ metrics logged - CV-average: fold_disagreement logged - Metric keys use slash-prefix (uq/total_uncertainty) - Per-volume values (not just mean)

task.name: Cross-fold consistency checks
task.description:
Add compute_cross_fold_consistency() that validates fold-level results are consistent. Checks:
1. No fold is a dramatic outlier (fold metric > 3*std from mean) 2. Fold rankings are stable (Kendall's W across folds) 3. ICC(2,1) > 0.5 for each metric (already in variance decomposition)
Flags inconsistent conditions for manual review before publication. This catches training bugs (e.g., a fold that diverged) early.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Stable folds pass all checks - Outlier fold is flagged - Kendall's W computed correctly (W=1 for perfect agreement) - Report format suitable for logging to MLflow

task.name: Post-hoc recalibration factor (p=2)
task.description:
Integrate temperature scaling as a factorial evaluation factor. For each checkpoint, the evaluation pipeline optionally applies temperature scaling before computing metrics.
Temperature scaling: 1. Find optimal T on calibration split (held-out from validation) 2. Apply: calibrated_probs = softmax(logits / T) 3. Re-compute all metrics on calibrated probabilities
The calibration split is the first 20% of validation volumes per fold. This is a simple split, not a separate fold.
p=2 factor: {none, temperature_scaling}

tdd_spec.tests: test_recalibration_none_identity test_recalibration_temperature_scaling test_recalibration_optimal_t_search test_recalibration_calibration_split test_recalibration_metrics_differ test_recalibration_mlflow_tag

task.acceptance_criteria: - None: metrics unchanged - Temperature scaling: optimal T found via NLL minimization - Calibration split: first 20% of validation volumes - Metrics recomputed on calibrated probabilities - MLflow tag: recalibration={none|temperature_scaling}

task.name: Specification curve figure
task.description:
Add _generate_specification_curve() to biostatistics_figures.py.
The specification curve (Simmons et al. 2011, Steegen et al. 2016) shows all conditions ranked by primary metric with binary indicator matrix below.
Layout: - Top panel: Y = clDice with 95% BCa bootstrap CI, X = rank (descending) - Bottom panel: Binary indicator matrix (rows = factors, columns = conditions) Rows: model (4), loss (3), aux_calib (2), post_training (3), recalibration (2), ensemble (5) - Color: highlight trusted metric; gray overlay for foil metric (DSC)
This is the most impactful single figure in the paper — shows at a glance which factor combinations drive performance.

tdd_spec.tests: test_specification_curve_generates_file test_specification_curve_ranking_order test_specification_curve_indicator_matrix test_specification_curve_ci_bands test_specification_curve_sidecar_json test_specification_curve_factor_rows

task.acceptance_criteria: - Figure saved as PNG + JSON sidecar - Conditions ranked by descending clDice - CI bands from BCa bootstrap - Indicator matrix has one row per factorial factor - Factor levels clearly labeled

task.name: Rank discrepancy table (Dice-as-foil)
task.description:
Add _generate_rank_discrepancy_table() to biostatistics_tables.py.
Columns: Model | Config | DSC | DSC Rank | clDice | clDice Rank | MASD | MASD Rank | Delta(DSC-clDice) Highlight: Red cells where |DSC rank - clDice rank| >= 2
This is the "smoking gun" for Analysis C (Dice-as-foil): Shows how Dice rankings diverge from topology-aware rankings.
task.acceptance_criteria: - Valid LaTeX with booktabs - Delta column = DSC_rank - clDice_rank - Large deltas (|delta| >= 2) highlighted with color command - All 6 models included (4 trainable + 2 zero-shot)

task.name: OpenLineage event emission for analysis flow
task.description: Wire LineageEmitter.pipeline_run() to analysis_flow.py. Inputs: checkpoints, evaluation_config Outputs: evaluation_metrics, ensemble_models, per_volume_scores
task.acceptance_criteria: - OpenLineage events emitted for analysis flow - Ensemble member datasets recorded as inputs
